description:
secretx-keyring/src/lib.rs:51-54: `pub struct KeyringBackend { service: String, account: String }` — no `Debug` derive. The /review-rusty checklist (Section 1, Type design) calls out 'Missing derives: Debug on almost everything'.

What goes wrong without Debug: a user trying to log/inspect their backend instance writes `println!("{:?}", store)` (or more commonly, the framework wraps it in a `tracing::debug!` call) and gets a compile error. The fields are not sensitive — `service` and `account` are URI components the user themselves provided and which appear in the URI string. There is no security argument against deriving Debug.

The sibling crate secretx-desktop has the same omission (DesktopKeyringBackend at secretx-desktop/src/lib.rs:43-46 also lacks Debug). Consistency argues for fixing both, but this finding is scoped to keyring.

Resolution: `#[derive(Debug)]` on the struct. One line.
title: Public type KeyringBackend missing #[derive(Debug)]
